summary: Bring Your Own Beamer (BYOB) is a one-night community event held in Yerevan on November 7, 2025, at Gabriel Sundukyan National Academic Theatre. Part of an international movement launched by Rafaël Rozendaal, this immersive audio-visual jam invites visual artists and musicians to showcase experimental work using their own projectors and installations without curators or selection processes. The event attracted 425 on-site participants and featured collaborative light installations in various formats including video, animation, VJ sets, mapping, and AR. Initiated locally by artist Misak Samokatian, BYOB Yerevan continues the tradition of spontaneous creative expression that has occurred in cities worldwide since 2010.

pros: Strong thematic clarity anchored in established international BYOB movement with proven track record across major cities
Clear local embedding through identified initiator (Misak Samokatian) and institutional venue partnership (Gabriel Sundukyan Theatre)
Concrete attendance outcome (425 on-site) demonstrates successful local reach
Radical openness format (no curators/judges) provides clear structural differentiator
cons: No artistic research layer or documented investigation process

pros: Strong integration into international BYOB network with confirmed partnership and validated attendance (425 on-site)
Connection to established global brand provides distribution channel for local artists
Completed event with concrete audience reach demonstrates execution capability
cons: No economic sustainability framework - clear revenue model or financial logic beyond budget
Employment dimension completely absent: no jobs created, paid roles, or meaningful work opportunities addressed
Financial innovation missing: standard funding approach with no novel mechanisms or structure
One-time event model with no evidence of sustained market access or artist career advancement

pros: No-gatekeeping open call structure removes barriers to artist participation
Documented community turnout of 425 attendees shows successful middle-scale engagement
Bilingual approach (Armenian/English) and international movement context
cons: No targeted inclusion of marginalized voices or underrepresented groups
Zero documented accessibility provisions across physical, economic, or digital dimensions
Audience engagement limited to passive viewing with no participatory mechanisms
Multilingual claim lacks implementation evidence or translation artifacts

pros: Strong execution of contemporary digital art forms with documented proof (video, photos)
Credible initiator with relevant track record at recognized venue
Clear participation mechanism and established international format precedent
Completed event demonstrates capacity to realize technical infrastructure
cons: Minimal critical or theoretical framing beyond participation format
No evidence of discourse development or public program
Single-person team structure with no documented production capacity

cons: No mechanisms or partnerships for intangible heritage engagement beyond format reference

Complete absence of environmental sensitivity considerations
